_about: Fallback pre-generated bio variants. Used for first paint (so the page works without JS) and during API outages. The live API endpoint (/api/bio) replaces these on dial change. Two lens × two length = 4 variants.

builder.short: Three times now: walked into a company with no design culture, built one, shipped through it. Bibel TV, ChurchDesk, Ninox. First design hires don't scare me; they're what I do.
builder.long: Three times now: walked into a company with no design culture, built one, shipped through it. At Ninox I joined the senior leadership team, managed a team of five including a design lead, set up shared design-system governance with engineering, and made the harder calls when someone wasn't the right fit. ChurchDesk and Bibel TV the same shape on different scales. Fifteen years of complex B2B SaaS underneath, lately back to hands-on so the next person I hire doesn't out-craft me.

ai-native.short: I design AI products and use AI to design. Right now: vibe-coding for databases, Copilot skills for sensitive support, agentic prototyping inside engineering. Hands-on, not from the sidelines.
ai-native.long: I design AI products and I use AI to design. At Ninox I shaped the vibe-coding onboarding directly with the founders — prompt-to-database with the schema rendering into the real editor, not a chat preview. At Bibel TV the same year I designed a Copilot skill for customer support (handbook as context, human keeps the send decision) and run agentic prototyping with engineering — Figma to Claude Code to working prototype the same week. The throughline: AI works best when its output lands on a surface the user can actually touch, not in a chat transcript about the artefact.
